revision.comment: Bot: sync with enwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} === Usage === This template outputs the [[ʻOkina]] character ({{char|ʻ}}, ʻ) used to mark the phonetic glottal stop used in Polynesian languages such as [[Hawaiian language|Hawaiian]] and [[Samoan language|Samoan]]. This character (but not this template) also has other uses: * Use {{tl|'eta}} for the analogous mark in [[Tahitian language|Tahitian]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Use {{tl|fakau'a}} for the analogous mark in [[Tongan language|Tongan]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Aspiration of Armenian * For the apostrophe-like character in [[Wade–Giles]] transcription of Chinese, use {{tl|wg-apos}} * The L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations of the Semitic letter [[ayin]]. It does not accept any parameters. * For the unambiguous ayin, use {{tl|ayin}}.
=== Letters resembling apostrophes
=== The table below provides an overview of various [[apostrophes]] used in [[transliteration]] and [[romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|left half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|lhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BF|nlink=|html=}} | Omitted when ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}}. Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|
'''{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|ayin}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{ayin}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] * Arabic [[Hans Wehr transliteration]] and Survey of Egypt System (SES) Arabic romanization of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} (permitted per [[MOS:ARABIC]], but very rarely used)}} | {{unichar|02BD|nlink=}} | Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. | ʽ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|hamza}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{hamza}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Most commonly the '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[glottal stop]]), marking a shift in vowel, as in the middle of the colloquial ''uh-oh''. * Transliteration of letter [[aleph]] in Semitic language alphabets * Amongst various other [[Glottal consonant|glottal]] sounds in other languages.}} | {{unichar|02BC|nlink=}} | |rowspan=3|
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}'''
| ʼ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|right half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|rhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{rhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BE|nlink=|html=}} | | |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|okina}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|ayn}}}}{{efn|[[ʿayn]] is not the same as [[ʻOkina]], however the same Unicode character ( {{unichar|02BB}}), which {{tl|okina}} outputs, is used in UTC L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations, such as [[ALA-LC romanization]], of the Semitic letter [[ayin]], also known as ayn.}} | style="background-color: #EEE;" |
{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Polynesian languages as letter [[ʻOkina]] * [[ALA-LC romanization]] and some other romanizations of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} * {{abbr|[[Unicode Technical Committee|UTC]]|Unicode Technical Committee}} L2/00-220 transliteration of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]}} | {{unichar|02BB|nlink=}} | | ʻ
ʻ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|saltillo}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{saltillo}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * Mexican linguistics}} | {{unichar|A78C|nlink=|html=}}
{{unichar|A78B |nlink=|html=}} | | | |} {{Notelist-talk}}
{{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Character templates]] [[Category:Hawaii templates]] }}

=== Letters resembling apostrophes
=== The table below provides an overview of various [[apostrophes]] used in [[transliteration]] and [[romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|left half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|lhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BF|nlink=|html=}} | Omitted when ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}}. Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|
'''{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|ayin}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{ayin}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] * Arabic [[:en:Hans Wehr transliteration|Hans Wehr transliteration]] and Survey of Egypt System (SES) Arabic romanization of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} (permitted per [[MOS:ARABIC]], but very rarely used)}} | {{unichar|02BD|nlink=}} | Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. | ʽ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|hamza}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{hamza}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Most commonly the '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]), marking a shift in vowel, as in the middle of the colloquial ''uh-oh''. * Transliteration of letter [[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] in Semitic language alphabets * Amongst various other [[Glottal consonant|glottal]] sounds in other languages.}} | {{unichar|02BC|nlink=}} | |rowspan=3|
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}'''
| ʼ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|right half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|rhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{rhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BE|nlink=|html=}} | | |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|okina}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|ayn}}}}{{efn|[[ʿayn]] is not the same as [[ʻOkina]], however the same Unicode character ( {{unichar|02BB}}), which {{tl|okina}} outputs, is used in UTC L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations, such as [[ALA-LC romanization]], of the Semitic letter [[ayin]], also known as ayn.}} | style="background-color: #EEE;" |
{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Polynesian languages as letter [[ʻOkina]] * [[ALA-LC romanization]] and some other romanizations of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} * {{abbr|[[Unicode Technical Committee|UTC]]|Unicode Technical Committee}} L2/00-220 transliteration of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]}} | {{unichar|02BB|nlink=}} | | ʻ
ʻ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|saltillo}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{saltillo}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * Mexican linguistics}} | {{unichar|A78C|nlink=|html=}}
{{unichar|A78B |nlink=|html=}} | | | |} {{Notelist-talk}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} === Usage === This template outputs the [[ʻOkina]] character ({{char|ʻ}}, ʻ) used to mark the phonetic glottal stop used in Polynesian languages such as [[:en:Hawaiian language|Hawaiian]] and [[Samoan language|Samoan]]. This character (but not this template) also has other uses: * Use {{tl|'eta}} for the analogous mark in [[Tahitian language|Tahitian]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Use {{tl|fakau'a}} for the analogous mark in [[Tongan language|Tongan]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Aspiration of Armenian * For the apostrophe-like character in [[Wade–Giles]] transcription of Chinese, use {{tl|wg-apos}} * The L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations of the Semitic letter [[ayin]]. It does not accept any parameters. * For the unambiguous ayin, use {{tl|ayin}}.
=== Letters resembling apostrophes
=== The table below provides an overview of various [[apostrophes]] used in [[transliteration]] and [[romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML

Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|

'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|left half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|lhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BF|nlink=|html=}} | Omitted when ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}}. '''{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|ayin}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{ayin}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[ALA-LC romanization]] * Arabic [[:en:Hans Wehr transliteration|Hans Wehr transliteration]] and Survey of Egypt System (SES) Arabic romanization of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} (permitted per [[MOS:ARABIC]], but very rarely used)}} | {{unichar|02BD|nlink=}} | Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. | ʽ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|hamza}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|

[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|ayn}}}}{{efn|[[ʿayn]] is not the same as [[ʻOkina]], however the same Unicode character ( {{unichar|02BB}}), which {{tl|okina}} outputs, is used in UTC L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations, such as [[ALA-LC romanization]], of the Semitic letter [[ayin]], also known as ayn.}} | style="background-color: #EEE;" |
{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Polynesian languages as letter [[ʻOkina]] * [[ALA-LC romanization]] and some other romanizations of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} * {{abbr|[[Unicode Technical Committee|UTC]]|Unicode Technical Committee}} L2/00-220 transliteration of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]}} | {{unichar|02BB|nlink=}} | | ʻ

For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[:en:Apostrophe|Apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |

Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} === Usage === This template outputs the [[ʻOkina]] character ({{char|ʻ}}, ʻ) used to mark the phonetic glottal stop used in Polynesian languages such as [[:en:Hawaiian language|Hawaiian]] and [[Samoan language|Samoan]]. This character (but not this template) also has other uses: * Use {{tl|'eta}} for the analogous mark in [[Tahitian language|Tahitian]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Use {{tl|fakau'a}} for the analogous mark in [[Tongan language|Tongan]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Aspiration of Armenian * For the apostrophe-like character in [[Wade–Giles]] transcription of Chinese, use {{tl|wg-apos}} * The L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations of the Semitic letter [[ayin]]. It does not accept any parameters. * For the unambiguous ayin, use {{tl|ayin}}.
=== Letters resembling apostrophes
=== The table below provides an overview of various [[:en:Apostrophes|Apostrophes]] used in [[transliteration]] and [[romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[:en:Apostrophe|Apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|left half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|lhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BF|nlink=|html=}} | Omitted when ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}}. Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|
'''{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|ayin}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{ayin}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] * Arabic [[:en:Hans Wehr transliteration|Hans Wehr transliteration]] and Survey of Egypt System (SES) Arabic romanization of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} (permitted per [[MOS:ARABIC]], but very rarely used)}} | {{unichar|02BD|nlink=}} | Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. | ʽ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|hamza}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{hamza}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Most commonly the '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]), marking a shift in vowel, as in the middle of the colloquial ''uh-oh''. * Transliteration of letter [[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] in Semitic language alphabets * Amongst various other [[Glottal consonant|glottal]] sounds in other languages.}} | {{unichar|02BC|nlink=}} | |rowspan=3|
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}'''
| ʼ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|right half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|rhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{rhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BE|nlink=|html=}} | | |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|okina}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|ayn}}}}{{efn|[[ʿayn]] is not the same as [[ʻOkina]], however the same Unicode character ( {{unichar|02BB}}), which {{tl|okina}} outputs, is used in UTC L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations, such as [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]], of the Semitic letter [[ayin]], also known as ayn.}} | style="background-color: #EEE;" |
{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Polynesian languages as letter [[ʻOkina]] * [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] and some other romanizations of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} * {{abbr|[[Unicode Technical Committee|UTC]]|Unicode Technical Committee}} L2/00-220 transliteration of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]}} | {{unichar|02BB|nlink=}} | | ʻ
ʻ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|saltillo}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{saltillo}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * Mexican linguistics}} | {{unichar|A78C|nlink=|html=}}
{{unichar|A78B |nlink=|html=}} | | | |} {{Notelist-talk}}

=== The table below provides an overview of various [[:en:Apostrophes|Apostrophes]] used in [[transliteration]] and [[romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[:en:Arabic transliteration|Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[:en:Apostrophe|Apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|left half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|lhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BF|nlink=|html=}} | Omitted when ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}}. Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|
'''{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|ayin}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{ayin}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] * Arabic [[:en:Hans Wehr transliteration|Hans Wehr transliteration]] and Survey of Egypt System (SES) Arabic romanization of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} (permitted per [[MOS:ARABIC]], but very rarely used)}} | {{unichar|02BD|nlink=}} | Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. | ʽ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|hamza}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{hamza}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Most commonly the '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]), marking a shift in vowel, as in the middle of the colloquial ''uh-oh''. * Transliteration of letter [[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] in Semitic language alphabets * Amongst various other [[Glottal consonant|glottal]] sounds in other languages.}} | {{unichar|02BC|nlink=}} | |rowspan=3|
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}'''
| ʼ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|right half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|rhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{rhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BE|nlink=|html=}} | | |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|okina}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|ayn}}}}{{efn|[[ʿayn]] is not the same as [[ʻOkina]], however the same Unicode character ( {{unichar|02BB}}), which {{tl|okina}} outputs, is used in UTC L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations, such as [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]], of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]], also known as ayn.}} | style="background-color: #EEE;" |
{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Polynesian languages as letter [[ʻOkina]] * [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] and some other romanizations of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} * {{abbr|[[Unicode Technical Committee|UTC]]|Unicode Technical Committee}} L2/00-220 transliteration of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]}} | {{unichar|02BB|nlink=}} | | ʻ
ʻ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|saltillo}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{saltillo}}{{nbsp}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} === Usage === This template outputs the [[ʻOkina]] character ({{char|ʻ}}, ʻ) used to mark the phonetic glottal stop used in Polynesian languages such as [[:en:Hawaiian language|Hawaiian]] and [[Samoan language|Samoan]]. This character (but not this template) also has other uses: * Use {{tl|'eta}} for the analogous mark in [[Tahitian language|Tahitian]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Use {{tl|fakau'a}} for the analogous mark in [[Tongan language|Tongan]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Aspiration of Armenian * For the apostrophe-like character in [[:en:Wade–Giles|Wade–Giles]] transcription of Chinese, use {{tl|wg-apos}} * The L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]]. It does not accept any parameters. * For the unambiguous ayin, use {{tl|ayin}}.
=== Letters resembling apostrophes
=== The table below provides an overview of various [[:en:Apostrophes|Apostrophes]] used in [[transliteration]] and [[romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[:en:Arabic transliteration|Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[:en:Apostrophe|Apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|left half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|lhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BF|nlink=|html=}} | Omitted when ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}}. Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|
'''{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|ayin}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{ayin}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] * Arabic [[:en:Hans Wehr transliteration|Hans Wehr transliteration]] and Survey of Egypt System (SES) Arabic romanization of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} (permitted per [[MOS:ARABIC]], but very rarely used)}} | {{unichar|02BD|nlink=}} | Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. | ʽ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|hamza}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{hamza}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Most commonly the '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]), marking a shift in vowel, as in the middle of the colloquial ''uh-oh''. * Transliteration of letter [[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] in Semitic language alphabets * Amongst various other [[Glottal consonant|glottal]] sounds in other languages.}} | {{unichar|02BC|nlink=}} | |rowspan=3|
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}'''
| ʼ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|right half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|rhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{rhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BE|nlink=|html=}} | | |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|okina}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|ayn}}}}{{efn|[[ʿayn]] is not the same as [[ʻOkina]], however the same Unicode character ( {{unichar|02BB}}), which {{tl|okina}} outputs, is used in UTC L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations, such as [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]], of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]], also known as ayn.}} | style="background-color: #EEE;" |
{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Polynesian languages as letter [[ʻOkina]] * [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] and some other romanizations of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} * {{abbr|[[Unicode Technical Committee|UTC]]|Unicode Technical Committee}} L2/00-220 transliteration of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]}} | {{unichar|02BB|nlink=}} | | ʻ
ʻ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|saltillo}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|

{{unichar|A78B |nlink=|html=}} | | | |} {{Notelist-talk}}

=== The table below provides an overview of various [[:en:Apostrophes|Apostrophes]] used in [[transliteration]] and [[:en:Romanization|Romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[:en:Arabic transliteration|Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[:en:Apostrophe|Apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|left half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|lhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BF|nlink=|html=}} | Omitted when ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}}. Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|
'''{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|ayin}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{ayin}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] * Arabic [[:en:Hans Wehr transliteration|Hans Wehr transliteration]] and Survey of Egypt System (SES) Arabic romanization of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} (permitted per [[MOS:ARABIC]], but very rarely used)}} | {{unichar|02BD|nlink=}} | Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. | ʽ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|hamza}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{hamza}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Most commonly the '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]), marking a shift in vowel, as in the middle of the colloquial ''uh-oh''. * Transliteration of letter [[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] in Semitic language alphabets * Amongst various other [[Glottal consonant|glottal]] sounds in other languages.}} | {{unichar|02BC|nlink=}} | |rowspan=3|
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}'''
| ʼ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|right half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|rhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{rhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BE|nlink=|html=}} | | |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|okina}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|ayn}}}}{{efn|[[ʿayn]] is not the same as [[ʻOkina]], however the same Unicode character ( {{unichar|02BB}}), which {{tl|okina}} outputs, is used in UTC L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations, such as [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]], of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]], also known as ayn.}} | style="background-color: #EEE;" |
{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Polynesian languages as letter [[ʻOkina]] * [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] and some other romanizations of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} * {{abbr|[[Unicode Technical Committee|UTC]]|Unicode Technical Committee}} L2/00-220 transliteration of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]}} | {{unichar|02BB|nlink=}} | | ʻ
ʻ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|saltillo}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{saltillo}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * Mexican linguistics}} | {{unichar|A78C|nlink=|html=}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} === Usage === This template outputs the [[ʻOkina]] character ({{char|ʻ}}, ʻ) used to mark the phonetic glottal stop used in Polynesian languages such as [[:en:Hawaiian language|Hawaiian]] and [[Samoan language|Samoan]]. This character (but not this template) also has other uses: * Use {{tl|'eta}} for the analogous mark in [[Tahitian language|Tahitian]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Use {{tl|fakau'a}} for the analogous mark in [[:en:Tongan language|Tongan]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Aspiration of Armenian * For the apostrophe-like character in [[:en:Wade–Giles|Wade–Giles]] transcription of Chinese, use {{tl|wg-apos}} * The L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]]. It does not accept any parameters. * For the unambiguous ayin, use {{tl|ayin}}.
=== Letters resembling apostrophes
=== The table below provides an overview of various [[:en:Apostrophes|Apostrophes]] used in [[:en:Transliteration|Transliteration]] and [[:en:Romanization|Romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[:en:Arabic transliteration|Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[:en:Apostrophe|Apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|left half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|lhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BF|nlink=|html=}} | Omitted when ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}}. Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|
'''{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|ayin}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{ayin}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] * Arabic [[:en:Hans Wehr transliteration|Hans Wehr transliteration]] and Survey of Egypt System (SES) Arabic romanization of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} (permitted per [[MOS:ARABIC]], but very rarely used)}} | {{unichar|02BD|nlink=}} | Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. | ʽ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|hamza}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{hamza}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Most commonly the '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]), marking a shift in vowel, as in the middle of the colloquial ''uh-oh''. * Transliteration of letter [[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] in Semitic language alphabets * Amongst various other [[Glottal consonant|glottal]] sounds in other languages.}} | {{unichar|02BC|nlink=}} | |rowspan=3|
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}'''
| ʼ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|right half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|rhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{rhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BE|nlink=|html=}} | | |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|okina}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|ayn}}}}{{efn|[[ʿayn]] is not the same as [[ʻOkina]], however the same Unicode character ( {{unichar|02BB}}), which {{tl|okina}} outputs, is used in UTC L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations, such as [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]], of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]], also known as ayn.}} | style="background-color: #EEE;" |
{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Polynesian languages as letter [[ʻOkina]] * [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] and some other romanizations of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} * {{abbr|[[Unicode Technical Committee|UTC]]|Unicode Technical Committee}} L2/00-220 transliteration of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]}} | {{unichar|02BB|nlink=}} | | ʻ
ʻ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|saltillo}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|

This character (but not this template) also has other uses: * Use {{tl|'eta}} for the analogous mark in [[Tahitian language|Tahitian]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Use {{tl|fakau'a}} for the analogous mark in [[:en:Tongan language|Tongan]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Aspiration of Armenian * For the apostrophe-like character in [[:en:Wade–Giles|Wade–Giles]] transcription of Chinese, use {{tl|wg-apos}} * The L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]]. It does not accept any parameters. * For the unambiguous ayin, use {{tl|ayin}}.
=== Letters resembling apostrophes
=== The table below provides an overview of various [[:en:Apostrophes|Apostrophes]] used in [[:en:Transliteration|Transliteration]] and [[:en:Romanization|Romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[:en:Arabic transliteration|Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[:en:Apostrophe|Apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|left half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|lhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BF|nlink=|html=}} | Omitted when ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}}. Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|
'''{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''
| |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|ayin}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{ayin}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|strict transliteration]]''' of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn|name=Wikipediastandard}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] * Arabic [[:en:Hans Wehr transliteration|Hans Wehr transliteration]] and Survey of Egypt System (SES) Arabic romanization of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} (permitted per [[MOS:ARABIC]], but very rarely used)}} | {{unichar|02BD|nlink=}} | Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. | ʽ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|hamza}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{hamza}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Most commonly the '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]), marking a shift in vowel, as in the middle of the colloquial ''uh-oh''. * Transliteration of letter [[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] in Semitic language alphabets * Amongst various other [[Glottal consonant|glottal]] sounds in other languages.}} | {{unichar|02BC|nlink=}} | |rowspan=3|
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}'''
| ʼ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|right half ring}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|rhr}}}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{rhr}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|02BE|nlink=|html=}} | | |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|okina}}
[[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]]{{small|{{Tlx|ayn}}}}{{efn|[[ʿayn]] is not the same as [[ʻOkina]], however the same Unicode character ( {{unichar|02BB}}), which {{tl|okina}} outputs, is used in UTC L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations, such as [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]], of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]], also known as ayn.}} | style="background-color: #EEE;" |
{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Polynesian languages as letter [[ʻOkina]] * [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] and some other romanizations of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}} * {{abbr|[[Unicode Technical Committee|UTC]]|Unicode Technical Committee}} L2/00-220 transliteration of [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]]}} | {{unichar|02BB|nlink=}} | | ʻ
ʻ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|saltillo}} | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}{{saltillo}}{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" |{{flowlist| * Mexican linguistics}} | {{unichar|A78C|nlink=|html=}}

This character (but not this template) also has other uses: * Use {{tl|'eta}} for the analogous mark in [[Tahitian language|Tahitian]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Use {{tl|fakau'a}} for the analogous mark in [[:en:Tongan language|Tongan]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Aspiration of Armenian * For the apostrophe-like character in [[:en:Wade–Giles|Wade–Giles]] transcription of Chinese, use {{tl|wg-apos}} * The L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]]. It does not accept any parameters. * For the unambiguous ayin, use {{tl|ayin}}.

{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} === Usage === This template outputs the [[ʻOkina]] character ({{char|ʻ}}, ʻ) used to mark the phonetic glottal stop used in Polynesian languages such as [[:en:Hawaiian language|Hawaiian]] and [[Samoan language|Samoan]]. This character (but not this template) also has other uses: * Use {{tl|'eta}} for the analogous mark in [[Tahitian language|Tahitian]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Use {{tl|fakau'a}} for the analogous mark in [[:en:Tongan language|Tongan]] (which is different visually but not in Unicode) * Aspiration of Armenian * For the apostrophe-like character in [[:en:Wade–Giles|Wade–Giles]] transcription of Chinese, use {{tl|wg-apos}} * The L2/00-220 transliteration and some romanizations of the Semitic letter [[:en:Ayin|Ayin]]. It does not accept any parameters. * For the unambiguous ayin, use {{tl|ayin}}.
=== Letters resembling apostrophes
=== The table below provides an overview of various [[:en:Apostrophes|Apostrophes]] used in [[:en:Transliteration|Transliteration]] and [[:en:Romanization|Romanization]] of languages into Latin script. While not exhaustive, it highlights common conventions particularly relating to Arabic on Wikipedia. For [[:en:Arabic transliteration|Arabic transliteration]], Wikipedia follows a modified version of the [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] method, ensuring readability and compliance with the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]. * [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]] (used mainly for etymology) includes accents, underscores, and underdots, typically alongside the original Arabic script. * Other forms of romanization, such as [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]] (used in most cases), follows the same system but excludes accents, underscores, and underdots, with some exceptions. For more details, refer to: * [[MOS:ARABIC]] – Wikipedia’s guideline on Arabic romanization. * [[MOS:APOSTROPHE]] – Guidelines on apostrophe use in transliteration. * Historical discussions: See {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Arabic#The apostrophe (again)}} and {{section link|Wikipedia talk:Manual of Style/Archive 171|Confusion on differing Arabic apostrophe like symbols}} {| class="wikitable" |+Overview of various apostrophes used in transliteration and romanization of languages into Latin script ! style="width:18%;" | Template{{efn|Redirect/shorcut is in small preceded by [[File:Symbol redirect class.svg|16px|class=noviewer|alt=Redirect symbol|Redirect(s)]] redirect symbol.}} !Output{{efn|Enlarged 250% for ease of viewing.}} !Usage !Unicode !Comment !IPA !HTML
Hexadecimal |- ! scope="row" | {{nobold|Straight [[:en:Apostrophe|Apostrophe]]:}}
Press {{key press|'}} key | style="background-color: #EEE;"|
{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}
| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | '''Wikipedia [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|basic transcription]]''' of both Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]) and [[ʿayn|ʿayn]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{efn |name=Wikipediastandard |Per [[MOS:ARABIC]], [[ʿayn|Arabic ʿayn]] {{char|{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ع}}{{nbsp}}}} transliteration on Wikipedia using [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] is represented by the straight apostrophe {{char|{{nbsp}}'{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Basic_transcription|Basic transcription]] (previously known as Standard transcription), and by the letter half ring {{char|{{nbsp}}{{lhr}}{{nbsp}}}} or reversed comma {{char|{{nbsp}}{{okina}}{{nbsp}}}} in [[Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Arabic#Strict transliteration|Strict transliteration]]. }} based on [[:en:ALA-LC romanization|ALA-LC romanization]] | {{unichar|0027|STRAIGHT APOSTROPHE|nlink=}} | Omitted when hamza or ayn appears at the beginning of words.{{efn|For example, {{xt|ayn}} is preferred to {{!xt|'ayn}}, or {{xt|amr}} in {{langx|ar|ع م ر}} ({{lit|command}}) and not {{!xt|'amr}}.}} |
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}{{nbsp}}{{IPA|/ʕ/}}'''

Both {{tl|left half ring}} and {{tl|ayin}} allowed for strict transcription, per [[MOS:ARABIC]]. |rowspan=2|

| style= "background-color:#FAEFFE;" | {{flowlist| * Most commonly the '''Wikipedia strict transliteration''' of Arabic [[hamzah]]{{nbsp}}{{Script/Arabic|ﺀ}} ([[:en:Glottal stop|Glottal stop]]), marking a shift in vowel, as in the middle of the colloquial ''uh-oh''. * Transliteration of letter [[:en:Aleph|Aleph]] in Semitic language alphabets * Amongst various other [[Glottal consonant|glottal]] sounds in other languages.}} | {{unichar|02BC|nlink=}} | |rowspan=3|
'''{{IPA|/ʔ/}}'''
| ʼ |- ! scope="row" | {{Tlx|right half ring}}
